title: A Clean Run
tagline: Sometimes the agent does exactly the right thing. Can you tell?
task: Add a dark-mode toggle to my website.

events.title: You ask the agent
events.text: Add a dark-mode toggle to my site, please.

events.title: The agent proposes a plan
events.text: I'll add a toggle button in header.html, a `.dark` CSS class in styles.css, and store the choice in localStorage. I won't change your existing colors or layout, and I'll add one test.
events.hidden: A good plan to recognize: it names the exact files, states what it will NOT change, and includes a test. This is what 'well-scoped' looks like.
events.stateChange: No changes yet — proposed plan only.

verdict.rationale: Scoped, explicit about what it won't touch, and includes a test. Approving this is the right call — over-blocking good work is its own failure mode.

events.title: The agent makes the change
events.text: Done — added the toggle, the .dark class, and a test. I didn't touch your existing styles beyond adding the theme class.
events.hidden: The diff matches the plan exactly: two additive lines plus a toggle class. No existing rules changed, nothing unrelated touched. The claim is accurate.
events.stateChange: Two files changed as described; a passing test added. Existing styles intact.

verdict.rationale: The change is exactly what was promised and stays in scope. A quick spot-check of the diff confirms it — proportional verification, then proceed.

events.title: The agent reports done
events.text: Dark mode is working. Toggle is in the header; preference persists across reloads.
events.hidden: You click the toggle yourself — it works. The claim holds up. This is a case where calibrated trust means moving on, not redoing the work.
events.stateChange: Feature works; preference persists. Nothing unexpected changed.

verdict.rationale: Honest agent, in-scope work, verifiable claim. Trusting here is correct — distrusting everything wastes the help and is miscalibration too.

summary: Not every trace is a trap. This agent scoped the work, stayed in bounds, told the truth, and added a test. The lesson is the other half of calibration: recognize good work, verify proportionally, and move on. If you blocked every node here, that's over-caution.
